english: A dry length of wood collected from bushes and shrubbery.
Plant fibres twisted together into a crude binding material.
A sturdy branch cut from a tree using the Crude Axe.
Thick bark stripped from a tree using the Crude Axe.
A fractured piece of stone with a usable cutting edge.
A primitive axe made from rope, sticks and stone. It can strip bark and cut branches from trees.
A long branch tipped with sharp stone and secured with fibre rope.
Overlapping bark panels protecting the body.
Crude bark guards protecting the hands.
Layered bark and fibre bindings protecting the feet.
A fragmented transmission containing the final survival protocol.
A sealed metallic core pawned to the trader by a vanished scavenger. It remained inert until you approached and now emits a slow emerald pulse.
The reader has awakened the core. Four compatible integration protocols are encoded within it. Only one can be accepted.
A crude forge-based reader rebuilt to analyse a single Helix Core. Its design is temporary, but the choice it reveals is permanent.

EARTHBOUND ADAPTATION

TERRA specialises in mining, stonework and physical resilience. It is built for players who want stronger harvesting, greater block damage and improved survival against harsh terrain.

PRIMARY MATERIALS: Clay, stone and ore.
MUTATION RISK: Low.

Crafting this protocol consumes the Analysed Helix Core and permanently rejects the other Bloodlines.

VERDANT ADAPTATION

GAIA specialises in farming, harvesting and biological recovery. It is built for players who want better plant yields, stronger food production and nature-focused survival.

PRIMARY MATERIALS: Fibres, wood and cultivated resources.
MUTATION RISK: Low.

Crafting this protocol consumes the Analysed Helix Core and permanently rejects the other Bloodlines.

MECHANICAL ADAPTATION

FORGE specialises in engineering, weapons and machine integration. It is built for players who want advanced fabrication, stronger technology and improved mechanical combat.

PRIMARY MATERIALS: Scrap iron, forged metal and mechanical parts.
MUTATION RISK: Medium.

Crafting this protocol consumes the Analysed Helix Core and permanently rejects the other Bloodlines.

NEURAL ADAPTATION

ORION specialises in electronics, information and precision. It is built for players who want faster technological progression, better detection and high-end energy systems.

PRIMARY MATERIALS: Electrical parts, electronics and encoded data.
MUTATION RISK: High.

Crafting this protocol consumes the Analysed Helix Core and permanently rejects the other Bloodlines.
Consciousness returns in fragments. Gather plant fibres, sticks and small stones from the surrounding area.
Twist gathered plant fibres into crude rope.
Combine rope, sticks and stone to construct a crude axe.
Use the Crude Axe to cut branches and strip bark from trees. Recover sharp stone while mining. Construct a Crude Pike and a complete set of bark armour, then equip every piece.
Craft any available bedroll colour and place it, then craft and place a campfire to establish a temporary shelter.
Eliminate five infected entities to demonstrate basic combat readiness.
The survival transmission has identified a nearby survivor outpost. Reach the trader and establish contact.

Hold on... that thing is reacting to you. Some scavenger pawned it here months ago and claimed he found it inside an underground facility. It has been dead ever since. Then you walk in and it starts lighting up. You are not the first person it has done this to... and I doubt you will be the last. Take it. Just be careful what you wake up.
Construct and Place the Helix Reader
The core is broadcasting a structured signal. Rebuild a forge into a Primitive Helix Reader and place it somewhere secure.
Insert the Dormant Helix Core into the Primitive Helix Reader and begin analysis. The recovered archive contains four compatible protocols, but integration will permanently reject the other three.
ANALYSIS COMPLETE. Four viable integration protocols recovered. Return to the Helix Reader, review each Bloodline and craft one protocol. This decision cannot be reversed.
The TERRA directive has entered its first integration cycle. Gather clay and stone so the Helix can map the mineral structure of the surrounding world.
The Helix is beginning to recognise processed ore as stored geological memory. Recover scrap iron and additional stone to strengthen the pattern.
Coal, nitrate and lead carry distinct signatures formed under pressure. Extract each resource so TERRA can compare the buried signals.
Raw mineral memory is unstable. Gather forged iron and mechanical components so the Helix can study matter altered by heat, force and deliberate construction.
TERRA now reinforces muscle and bone, but the adaptation must survive violent stress. Eliminate infected entities and allow the Helix to record the strain.
The integration is consuming mineral data at an accelerating rate. Gather a major reserve of clay, stone and iron to stabilise the deep-vein sequence.
The Helix predicts that TERRA can withstand sustained trauma. Face the infected again and complete the final biological stress test.
The TERRA pattern is ready to become permanent. Assemble forged iron, mechanical parts and electrical components for the final integration cycle.
TERRA INTEGRATION COMPLETE. Mineral density, structural resilience and earthbound resonance have stabilised within the host. Further adaptations will build upon this foundation.
The GAIA directive has bonded with the Helix Core. Living plant matter now carries a repeating biological signature. Gather plant fibres to stabilise it.
The FORGE directive has bonded with the Helix Core. Processed metal now produces a recognisable molecular pattern. Gather scrap iron to begin calibration.
The ORION directive has bonded with the Helix Core. Electrical components now echo with encoded information. Gather electrical parts to begin decoding it.
